privileged_info_summary: WASP-12 is an F-type star at RA 06h 30m 32.794s, Dec +29° 40′ 20.29″, hosting ultra-hot Jupiter WASP-12b in a 1.091420 day orbit. The planet causes transits with ~1.4% depth (14 millimagnitudes) and 2.7 hour duration. WASP-12b has radius 1.79 ± 0.09 Jupiter radii; host star radius is 1.57-1.69 solar radii (task claims 1.599, likely incorrect). The AstroImageJ example dataset (~4.3 GB, 200-230 calibrated FITS images in r-band) is from University of Louisville using the MORC24 telescope. Task metadata claims 186 images, contradicting documented ~230 science images in the publicly available dataset.
